location.office_time_required: majority of work remote; in-person onsites once or twice per quarter for 2-4 consecutive days

level_signals: 5-7 years of product management experience required
Comp range $187,900–$260,000 USD base, consistent with Principal/Staff IC
Owns strategy for one of the most critical systems at Upstart

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: BA/BS in Economics, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.
Experience building or managing backend platforms or infrastructure systems
Experience working with engineering teams on large-scale, complex systems
Experience defining product requirements and driving execution for technical products

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working on pricing systems, ads platforms, or similar optimization systems
Experience evolving monolithic systems into modular or service-oriented architectures
Experience working in machine learning, data science, or analytics-driven environments
Experience operating in highly cross-functional environments with multiple stakeholders

explicitly_excluded: Quebec-based candidates (Canada roles): 'For roles based in Canada, please note that we are not currently able to hire in Quebec.'

scope.surface: Pricing Platform — core primitives translating application data into loan offers (APR) across unsecured, auto, and home loan products; covering eligibility, qualification, underwriting, and offer selection
scope.business_model: B2B2C AI lending marketplace — partners with banks and credit unions to deliver consumer lending
primary_metrics_inferred: Reliability and availability of pricing systems
Scalability of platform across multiple loan products
Reduction of high-impact failures in pricing systems
core_responsibilities: Define and execute the long-term vision for Upstart's pricing platform, enabling scalable and reusable capabilities across multiple loan products
Improve the reliability, quality, and availability of pricing systems, reducing risk and preventing high-impact failures in a business-critical system
Drive platform evolution from a complex, monolithic architecture toward modular, service-oriented systems that increase engineering velocity
Partner with engineering, machine learning, and product teams across unsecured, auto, and home lending to enable new pricing capabilities and use cases
Balance competing priorities across stakeholders, making principled tradeoffs between short-term business needs and long-term platform investments
Identify and unlock opportunities to accelerate product development by improving platform primitives and reducing dependency bottlenecks

compensation.equity_form: annual equity grants that vest quarterly; Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP) with discounted stock purchase options

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($187,900–$260,000 USD base)
Equity compensation and bonus explicitly mentioned
Fully remote-eligible across US (and Canada outside Quebec)
Clear product surface with well-defined ownership (pricing platform, not vague)
Generous 401(k) match ($2 for every $1, up to $15,000 annually)
In-person cadence is lightweight (once or twice per quarter)
Team operates across East/West coast time zones — broad flexibility
red_flags: Work authorization and visa sponsorship not explicitly stated — unclear for non-US/non-Canadian applicants
Canada-specific salary range is in CAD ($182,800–$230,000 CAD), which is significantly lower in USD terms than the US range
Location pay differential applies — actual base may be lower than the top of the stated range depending on geography
Role requires very specific platform/infrastructure PM background which narrows the candidate pool considerably

location.work_auth (not explicitly stated; set to no-sponsorship based on absence of sponsorship mention and US/Canada only hiring)
